title: Ken Saro-Wiwa's Unread Statement to the Tribunal
author: Ken Saro-Wiwa
body:
We all stand before history. I am a man of peace, of ideas. Appalled by the denigrating poverty of my people who live on a richly endowed land, distressed by their political marginalisation and economic strangulation, angered by the devastation of their land, their ultimate heritage, anxious to preserve their right to life and to a decent living, and determined to usher to this country as a whole a fair and just democratic system which protects everyone and every ethnic group and gives us all a valid claim to human civilisation, I have devoted my intellectual and material resources, my very life, to a cause in which I have total belief and from which I cannot be blackmailed or intimidated.

I have no doubt at all about the ultimate success of my cause, no matter what the trials and tribulations of the moment. Neither imprisonment nor death can stop our ultimate victory.

[Ken Saro-Wiwa was prevented from reading this statement in court. It was later published by his family and supporters. — *Archive note*]

title: The Ogoni Bill of Rights (Excerpt)
author: MOSOP
body:
We, the people of Ogoni, being a separate and distinct people in the Federal Republic of Nigeria, wish to draw the attention of the Nigerian Government and the international community to our plight under the current Federal Republic of Nigeria, and to appeal for help and action.

The Ogoni people have explored and exploited their resources for the benefit of the country as a whole without reciprocal attention to the development of the Ogoni area. In spite of the oil wealth produced by the Ogoni people, the Ogoni area remains underdeveloped.

Whereas the Ogoni people wish to manage their own affairs, we demand political autonomy to participate in the affairs of the Republic as a distinct and separate unit by whatever name called, provided that this is achieved without compromising the democratic ideals of the Federal Republic.

[This is an excerpt from the publicly available text of the Ogoni Bill of Rights, 1990.]

title: Letter from Ken Saro-Wiwa to Greenpeace
author: Ken Saro-Wiwa
body: [PLACEHOLDER — A letter written by Ken Saro-Wiwa from detention to Greenpeace UK is held in the Saro-Wiwa family archive. Reproduction pending permission from the estate. This document will be replaced with the authenticated text upon clearance. The letter describes conditions in Port Harcourt Prison and appeals for continued international solidarity with the Ogoni cause.]

title: Testimony of Witness Who Recanted
author: Archive note
body: [PLACEHOLDER — A sworn affidavit from a prosecution witness who stated he had been paid to testify against Ken Saro-Wiwa and the other defendants was widely reported in the Nigerian and international press following the executions. A full reproduction of the affidavit text, sourced from the public record, will be placed here once it has been authenticated by the archive team.]

title: Ken Saro-Wiwa at an MOSOP rally, circa 1993
author: Photographer unknown
body: [PLACEHOLDER — Archival photograph. Image rights are being sought. When placed, this image will display the document-develop reveal animation: slightly blurred and warm-toned on load, sharpening to full clarity over 1.4 seconds.]

title: Africa Kills Her Sun (Excerpt)
author: Ken Saro-Wiwa
body:
[This poem, written in the form of a letter from a man awaiting execution, was first published in Ken Saro-Wiwa's collection 'A Forest of Flowers' (1986) and later widely anthologised. The full text is under copyright held by the Saro-Wiwa estate. An excerpt appears here for educational purposes under fair dealing / educational use provisions. Full rights will be pursued through the estate for archive inclusion.]

*On the morning of my death I see the sun rise*
*and wonder at the world's indifference...*

[Excerpt. Full text pending estate clearance.]
